response: The American rock band Sugar Ray gained mainstream fame in the 1990s. Sugar Ray, formed in 1986, started as a funk metal band and gained popularity in 1997 with the release of the song "Fly," which helped them transition to a mainstream, pop rock sound. Their album "Fly" was a significant success, and the band achieved notable chart placements, including number 17 on the Billboard 200. In contrast, Against the Current, an American pop rock band formed in 2011, did not gain mainstream fame in the 1990s.
